page: Microsoft Azure F64als_v6 is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the Falsv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration provides 128.0 GB of RAM, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This low memory-to-vCPU ratio classifies the instance as compute-optimized. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark results place the F64als_v6 in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and physics tests. It also achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier performance for read-only small block cached operations and large block write operations, while large block read-only bandwidth is in the top 25%. However, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at an average level (middle 50%).

The primary tradeoff of the F64als_v6 is its low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory data processing. Despite this, it demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and medium-model LLM text generation. The instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that require high raw CPU throughput and vector processing capabilities—supported by AVX-512 and cryptography instruction sets—without requiring large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and compute-heavy batch processing.
description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v6 is a compute-optimized x86_64 server instance from the Falsv6 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier (top 10%) performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. However, single-threaded compression tasks and large-model LLM prompt processing remain average. The primary tradeoff is the limited memory capacity relative to the high core count. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and compilation performance but features a low 2.0 GB per core memory ratio.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64als_v6 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier compute performance for CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for intensive CPU workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores at 3.7 GHz.
128.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per core memory density.
Top-tier benchmark performance in multi-core CPU tasks, software compilation, and ray tracing.
No bundled local storage or GPUs, with support for external Premium Storage.
Best suited for compute-bound workloads, batch processing, and image rendering.
